children.name: ParseCommand
children.purpose: Parse the incoming JSON input to extract the command type and parameters
children.behavior: Extract command field from input JSON and validate its presence
postconditions: command is one of the four allowed values
guarantees: Returns error if command missing or invalid

inputs.name: input
inputs.description: Raw JSON input from user containing command and parameters

outputs.name: params
outputs.description: Remaining parameters from input after extracting command

constraints.description: Input must be valid JSON
Command field must be present and one of: send, history, create_channel, join

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid JSON with command field, returns command and params correctly
Given invalid JSON, returns error
Given missing command field, returns error
Given invalid command value, returns error

children.name: ValidateSendMessage
children.purpose: Validate send message request: check channel membership and message content
children.behavior: Verify user is member of target channel and message is non-empty
postconditions: Returns true only if user is member and message non-empty
guarantees: Does not modify any data store

inputs.name: channels
inputs.description: Channel data store for membership check

outputs.name: validation_result
outputs.description: Boolean indicating if send is allowed

children.signature: def ValidateSendMessage(params: dict, channels: dict) -> tuple[bool, str]

data_operations.description: Read channel data to check membership

constraints.description: Must not modify channels data store
Message must be non-empty string
User must be member of the channel

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid params and user is member, returns (True, '')
Given empty message, returns (False, error message)
Given user not member of channel, returns (False, error message)
Given non-existent channel, returns (False, error message)

children.name: StoreMessage
children.purpose: Store a new message in the messages data store
children.behavior: Append message with timestamp and user info to messages store
postconditions: Message is appended to messages store
guarantees: Message is stored with unique id and current timestamp

outputs.name: stored_message
outputs.description: The stored message object with id and timestamp

children.signature: def StoreMessage(params: dict, messages: list) -> dict

data_operations.description: Append new message to messages store

constraints.description: Must generate unique message id
Must include current timestamp
Must not modify channels data store

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid params, message is appended to messages store
Stored message contains id, channel_id, user_id, message, timestamp fields
Message id is unique across all messages

children.name: RetrieveHistory
children.purpose: Retrieve recent message history for a channel, limited to 100 messages
children.behavior: Query messages store for channel_id, return last 100 messages in chronological order
postconditions: Returns array of messages sorted by timestamp

outputs.name: history
outputs.description: Array of up to 100 message objects

children.signature: def RetrieveHistory(params: dict, messages: list) -> list

constraints.description: Must not modify any data store
Must return at most 100 messages
Must return messages sorted by timestamp ascending

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid channel_id, returns messages for that channel
Returns at most 100 messages
Messages are sorted by timestamp ascending
Given non-existent channel_id, returns empty list

children.name: CreateChannel
children.purpose: Create a new channel in the channels data store
children.behavior: Add new channel entry with unique id and name, initialize member list with creator
postconditions: New channel exists in channels store
guarantees: Creator is added as first member

outputs.name: created_channel
outputs.description: The new channel object with id and members

children.signature: def CreateChannel(params: dict, channels: dict) -> dict

data_operations.description: Add new channel to channels store

constraints.description: Channel name must be non-empty
Channel name must be unique
Must generate unique channel id
Creator must be added as first member

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid params, new channel is created in channels store
Created channel contains id, name, members fields
Creator is included in members list
Given duplicate channel name, returns error
Given empty channel name, returns error

children.name: JoinChannel
children.purpose: Add a user to an existing channel's member list
children.behavior: Update channels store to include user_id in channel's members array
preconditions: channel exists and user not already member
postconditions: User is added to channel's member list

inputs.name: channels
inputs.description: Channels data store for reading and writing

children.signature: def JoinChannel(params: dict, channels: dict) -> bool

data_operations.description: Read channel data and update member list

constraints.description: Channel must exist
User must not already be a member
Must not modify messages data store

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid params and channel exists, user is added to member list
Given non-existent channel, returns False
Given user already member, returns False
Does not create duplicate membership

children.name: FormatResponse
children.purpose: Format the final output JSON with success status, data, and message
children.behavior: Construct response object based on command execution result
postconditions: Output is valid JSON with expected structure
guarantees: Response always contains success, data, message fields

outputs.name: output
outputs.description: Formatted JSON response with success, data, message fields

children.signature: def FormatResponse(success: bool, data: any, message: str) -> dict

constraints.description: Output must be valid JSON
Output must contain success, data, message fields

acceptance_criteria.description: Given success=True, data, message, returns JSON with success: true
Given success=False, data, message, returns JSON with success: false
Output always contains success, data, message fields

governance_notes: All children are pure functions or atomic operations. Data store access is limited to read/write as specified. No child has global variables. The parent orchestrator is responsible for routing data between children and managing the overall flow. Each child's data operations are scoped to only the data stores they need, and no child accesses data stores unnecessarily.
